name: Mjaddra (Lentils with Rice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean and wash the lentils. Boil them in water until tender.
2. Mash the cooked lentils and strain into a separate pot.
3. Add the rice, salt, and spices to the lentils. Mix well.
4. In a pan, sauté the chopped onions in oil until golden brown.
5. Add the sautéed onions with the cooking oil to the lentil mixture.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil with enough water to cover ingredients.
7. Simmer over low heat for about 10 minutes, stirring regularly to prevent sticking.
8. Serve lukewarm or cold with radishes and spring onions, or a cabbage salad.
That's the recipe straight from the book. I make a couple of changes: first, I'm liberal with the cumin and allspice (bhar); I don't use measuring spoons and likely use more than 1/4 or 1/2 teaspoon. The bhar is described as a spice blend made of cloves, cinnamon, and nutmeg, similar in flavor to allspice, which can be substituted.
I use brown basmati rice, so it requires boiling for longer than 10 minutes; I add more water regularly to prevent the rice from drying out. I plan to visit the restaurant where I first had this dish and ask them about their spice usage, as theirs is spicier and darker than mine.
Food For The Vegetarian - Traditional Lebanese Recipes
Posted by: bobp@msi.com (Bob Pitha)
